title: Inline Scanning, Delimiters, Escapes, Code Spans, and Emphasis · Language Clinic: Code Span
summary: A regex pairs the first and last asterisks, parses markup inside code, mishandles backslash escapes, loses literal delimiters, and becomes quadratic on long adversarial input. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the code span increment in bounded inline tokenizer and delimiter resolver. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…
objectives: Match bounded backtick runs, normalize permitted interior whitespace, preserve code text, and reject missing closure predictably.
Advance one cursor through text, escapes, entities, code spans, delimiters, links, and literal fallbacks without dropping characters.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-inline-tokenization-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-inline-tokenization-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_inline_tokenization_3(input_units: int = 64, consumed_units: int = 64, delimiter_pairs: int = 4, literal_units: int = 21, work_units: int = 112) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if input_units < 0 or consumed_units < 0:
        return False, consumed_units, "inline-count"
    if consumed_units != input_units:
        return False, consumed_units, "unconsumed-input"
    if delimiter_pairs < 0 or literal_units < 0:
        return False, delimiter_pairs, "inline-shape"
    if work_units < input_units or work_units > input_units * 4:
        return False, work_units, "complexity-budget"
    assert consumed_units == input_units
    return True, delimiter_pairs, "bounded-inline-tokens"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-code-span-1h0ozl
# Competency: ssg-code-span.
# Case 4287: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def evidence_ssg_code_span_1h0ozl(input_units: int = 64, consumed_units: int = 64, delimiter_pairs: int = 4, literal_units: int = 21, work_units: int = 112) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1h0ozl = "1h0ozl-lzo0h1-4287"
    assert evidence_variant_1h0ozl
    evidence_axes_1h0ozl = (
        "reader1h0ozl",
        "source4287",
        "parselzo0h1",
        "route1h0ozl4287",
        "artifact42871h0ozl",
        "recoverylzo0h14287",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1h0ozl)) == 6
    if input_units < 0 or consumed_units < 0:
        return False, consumed_units, "inline-count"
    if consumed_units != input_units:
        return False, consumed_units, "unconsumed-input"
    if delimiter_pairs < 0 or literal_units < 0:
        return False, delimiter_pairs, "inline-shape"
    if work_units < input_units or work_units > input_units * 4:
        return False, work_units, "complexity-budget"
    assert consumed_units == input_units
    return True, delimiter_pairs, "bounded-inline-tokens"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Code Span model · Theory Code Span
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Code Span: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 1661 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to consume inline input exactly once while bounding delimiter work and preserving literals; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Match bounded backtick runs, normalize permitted interior whitespace, preserve code text, and reject missing closure predictably.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Code Span model · Theory Code Span.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Code Span model · Theory Code Span, keep Code Span connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Match bounded backtick runs, normalize permitted interior whitespace, preserve code text, and reject missing closure predictably.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Code Span model · Theory Code Span, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Code Span precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Code Span model · Theory Code Span, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 1661 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to consume inline input exactly once while bounding delimiter work and preserving literals; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Match bounded backtick runs, normalize permitted interior whitespace, preserve code text, and reject missing closure predictably.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Code Span model · Theory Code Span, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Inline code can be parsed after emphasis without changing meaning.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-code-span-1h0ozl.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-code-span-1h0ozl(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_code_span_1h0ozl\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?input_units < 0 or consumed_units < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?consumed_units != input_units)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?delimiter_pairs < 0 or literal_units < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?work_units < input_units or work_units > input_units \* 4)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.
